verses.text: Sanballat and Tobiah jeer at the builders — 'will they revive the stones?' — sneering that a fox climbing the wall would topple it. Mockery is the enemy's first weapon. Scorn is meant to shame God's people into laying down the work.

Sanballat and Tobiah jeer at the builders — 'will they revive the stones?' — sneering that a fox climbing the wall would topple it. Mockery is the enemy's first weapon. Scorn is meant to shame God's people into laying down the work.

Sanballat and Tobiah jeer at the builders — 'will they revive the stones?' — sneering that a fox climbing the wall would topple it. Mockery is the enemy's first weapon. Scorn is meant to shame God's people into laying down the work.

Nehemiah does not trade insults but prays, 'hear, O our God; for we are despised,' turning their reproach back on them. He carries the offence to God. The wise answer to contempt is not retaliation but committing the matter to the Judge of all.

Nehemiah does not trade insults but prays, 'hear, O our God; for we are despised,' turning their reproach back on them. He carries the offence to God. The wise answer to contempt is not retaliation but committing the matter to the Judge of all.
'So built we the wall... for the people had a mind to work.' A willing heart makes the work go forward. When God's people set their minds to it, mockery cannot stop the rising of the wall.

As the enemies conspire to attack, Nehemiah and the people make their prayer and set a watch day and night. They join petition to precaution. Faith prays to God and also keeps guard, trusting Him without tempting Him.

As the enemies conspire to attack, Nehemiah and the people make their prayer and set a watch day and night. They join petition to precaution. Faith prays to God and also keeps guard, trusting Him without tempting Him.

As the enemies conspire to attack, Nehemiah and the people make their prayer and set a watch day and night. They join petition to precaution. Faith prays to God and also keeps guard, trusting Him without tempting Him.

Judah's strength flags amid the rubble, the enemies threaten, and those living near them sound the alarm again and again. Discouragement within meets danger without. The middle of a great work is often its hardest hour, when weariness and fear press together.

Judah's strength flags amid the rubble, the enemies threaten, and those living near them sound the alarm again and again. Discouragement within meets danger without. The middle of a great work is often its hardest hour, when weariness and fear press together.

Judah's strength flags amid the rubble, the enemies threaten, and those living near them sound the alarm again and again. Discouragement within meets danger without. The middle of a great work is often its hardest hour, when weariness and fear press together.

Nehemiah arms the people by families and charges them, 'be not afraid... remember the Lord, which is great and terrible, and fight for your brethren.' He answers fear with the greatness of God. Remembering whom we serve steadies the hand for the home and kindred we defend.

Nehemiah arms the people by families and charges them, 'be not afraid... remember the Lord, which is great and terrible, and fight for your brethren.' He answers fear with the greatness of God. Remembering whom we serve steadies the hand for the home and kindred we defend.

The plot foiled, half the men work while half stand guard, and the builders labour with one hand and hold a weapon in the other. Work and watchfulness go together. The Lord's servants build with diligence even while ready to face the foe.

The plot foiled, half the men work while half stand guard, and the builders labour with one hand and hold a weapon in the other. Work and watchfulness go together. The Lord's servants build with diligence even while ready to face the foe.

The plot foiled, half the men work while half stand guard, and the builders labour with one hand and hold a weapon in the other. Work and watchfulness go together. The Lord's servants build with diligence even while ready to face the foe.

The plot foiled, half the men work while half stand guard, and the builders labour with one hand and hold a weapon in the other. Work and watchfulness go together. The Lord's servants build with diligence even while ready to face the foe.

Nehemiah tells them, 'the work is great... in what place ye hear the trumpet, resort thither: our God shall fight for us.' They are scattered yet ready to rally. God's people, though spread thin, are one body, and He Himself fights for them.

Nehemiah tells them, 'the work is great... in what place ye hear the trumpet, resort thither: our God shall fight for us.' They are scattered yet ready to rally. God's people, though spread thin, are one body, and He Himself fights for them.

From dawn till the stars appear they labour, never undressing, weapons always at hand. Their devotion spares no comfort. A great work for God claims wholehearted, unwearied effort, holding nothing back until it is done.
